meaning: adj.织成锦缎的,穿锦缎的,用浮雕法织锦的
examples: He was wrapped in a velvet dressing-gown and sat against a big brocaded cushion.
It is lined with white and worn over a white brocaded gown.
When the painting is done, it is mounted on a brocaded silk border.
He traveled, though less extensively than John Paul, and presided over Masses that were heavy on Latin, Gregorian chant and the silk brocaded vestments of his pre-Vatican II predecessors.
